name: RYOBI 40V HP Brushless 15" Cordless String Trimmer (RY40290VNM)

specs.Battery: Compatible with RYOBI 40V (NOT 18V ONE+ — different system)

whatWorks: Integrates with the RYOBI 40V ecosystem — buyers who own the RYOBI 40V HP Brushless mower or 40V blower share batteries across tools without extra chargers or purchase cost.
At ~6.7 lb with battery, it's slightly lighter than the EGO ST1521S — a small but noticeable difference during extended edge trimming along long fence lines or driveways.
Variable speed trigger gives control for different tasks — slow for careful trimming near garden beds where flying debris could damage plants, fast for open overgrowth along fences.
15" cutting swath handles typical residential edging and trimming at a pace appropriate for urban and suburban Canadian lots.
worthKnowing: RYOBI 40V and RYOBI 18V ONE+ batteries are physically different and not interchangeable. This is one of the most common buyer errors — many RYOBI 18V users assume compatibility and are disappointed. Confirm you're buying 40V.

.080" line diameter is lighter than EGO's .095" — it handles standard grass and light weeds well but will struggle with thicker brush or heavy overgrowth. Line replacement spools in .080" are widely available.
Battery inclusion varies by listing — tool-only models are common. Existing 40V users get excellent value; new buyers must budget for a 40V battery and charger separately.
Not compatible with RYOBI attachment accessories the way EGO's split shaft is — this is a straight shaft trimmer with no attachment expansion option.

commonComplaints.title: 40V and 18V batteries are not…
commonComplaints.body: 40V and 18V batteries are not compatible — I wasted a week thinking my 18V ONE+ batteries would work in this trimmer. They look different but I didn't notice until I tried to charge.

commonComplaints.title: Line diameter is lighter than EGO…
commonComplaints.body: Line diameter is lighter than EGO — struggles on thicker weeds along my back fence. Fine for regular maintenance trimming though.

commonComplaints.title: Battery not included in tool-only listing…
commonComplaints.body: Battery not included in tool-only listing — make sure you already have a 40V battery. The kit version is the safer purchase.

commonComplaints.title: Can't handle heavy overgrowth after…
commonComplaints.body: Can't handle heavy overgrowth after a missed week in August. Had to make two passes. EGO would handle it in one.

verdict: The RYOBI 40V HP Brushless string trimmer is the right choice for buyers already in the RYOBI 40V ecosystem — battery sharing with the mower and blower makes the total cost compelling. For fresh buyers comparing it to the EGO ST1521S, the lighter .080" line, lack of attachment compatibility, and similar price make the EGO the stronger standalone purchase. The 40V/18V battery confusion is genuinely common and worth flagging clearly before any RYOBI purchase.

realBuyers.quote: Already own a RYOBI 40V mower and blower — this trimmer added to my setup for under $150 and uses the same batteries. No-brainer.
Lightweight and easy to handle for extended edge trimming. My older corded trimmer was exhausting by comparison.
Clean edge along my fence line on the first pass. Works exactly as expected for a weekly maintenance trimmer.
Easy line loading once you figure it out. The bump head works reliably on my third season.

sources.title: Best Cordless String Trimmers — This Old House

metaDescription: RYOBI 40V HP Brushless 15" string trimmer review for Canada: 40V ecosystem compatible, .080" dual line, variable speed. Honest on 40V vs 18V battery confusion. ~$149 CAD.
introText: The RYOBI 40V HP Brushless 15" String Trimmer is designed for the RYOBI 40V battery ecosystem — sharing batteries with RYOBI 40V mowers and blowers. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B09FN5M4PG) at around $149 CAD.
